title: Bridging Design Systems and Code with MCP and AI Agents
summary: Will Scott explains how design systems and context engineering guide intelligent agents in software design. The discussion covers how MCP and AI agents work together to build consistent, correct applications with structured design guidance.
writeup: Will Scott walks through how design systems and context engineering steer AI agents during software design, using the Model Context Protocol (MCP) to connect the two. The video matters because consistency and correctness in generated applications depend on the structured guidance feeding the agents, not just the model itself. Key takeaways: design systems supply the rules and components, context management shapes what the agent sees, and MCP links agents to that design guidance so the resulting applications stay consistent and correct.
